---
title: "Interactive App (Shinylive)"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Interactive App (Shinylive)}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

## Try monitOS in your browser

The monitOS Shiny app is available as a **Shinylive** application that runs
entirely in your browser using WebAssembly — no R installation or server
required.

<iframe
  src="https://nicaise.github.io/monitOS/shinylive-app/"
  style="border: 1px solid #dee2e6; border-radius: 4px; width: 100%; height: 800px;"
  allow="cross-origin-isolated"
  loading="lazy"
  title="monitOS Shinylive App">
</iframe>

<noscript>
<p>
  The interactive app requires JavaScript. Please enable JavaScript in your
  browser or visit the
  <a href="https://nicaise.github.io/monitOS/shinylive-app/">standalone app</a>
  directly.
</p>
</noscript>

## About Shinylive

[Shinylive](https://shinylive.io) runs Shiny applications entirely in the
browser using [WebAssembly](https://webassembly.org/) via the
[webR](https://docs.r-wasm.org/webr/latest/) project. This means:
- **No server required** — the app runs on your device
- **No installation needed** — just open the page
- **Privacy** — your inputs never leave your browser

The app implements the same `bounds()` function available in the R package.
For programmatic access, install monitOS from CRAN:

```r
install.packages("monitOS")
library(monitOS)
bounds(
  events = c(60, 89, 110, 131, 178),
  power_int = 0.9,
  falsepos = 0.025,
  hr_null = 1.3,
  hr_alt = 0.8,
  rand_ratio = 1
)
```

## Local Shiny App

If you have R and monitOS installed, you can also run the full Shiny dashboard
locally:

```r
monitOS::run_app()
```
